text: c'est pas de la fuite — c'est de l'entretien.
author: Jacques Gautreau, Casque

text: Colour with a footnote is colour you remember.
author: Storied Colors, via Sidebar

text: Don't write for an audience. Picture one specific person — often yourself three years ago — and write to them.
author: Julia Evans

text: Effort is a signal of care, and care is what attention is actually paid for.
author: Tom Bedor

text: Enthusiasts are betting the tools mature before the bill comes due, skeptics are betting the slop accumulates faster than anyone can shovel it.
author: null

text: Focus isn't a discipline you summon; it's an architecture you defend, and most environments are quietly designed to take it from you.
author: Sidebar

text: I didn't plant it, but I get the view, and some mornings I wonder if that's the bargain we make with beauty. Somebody tends the ground, and the rest of us just happen to be standing where the light falls.
author: Jacques Gautreau

text: I've been trying to learn that. To stop and hold. To trust what the body knows before the mind names it.
author: Jacques Gautreau, “Puck” (Pomodoro Poems)

text: Je mets ma confiance dans le petit à petit, dans le 1% de mieux qu'hier.
author: From \"1%\"

text: Knowing changes hearing.
author: After Wu and Holmes

text: Most creativity is curation.
author: Linear, “Output isn't design”

text: Parce avant de faire, il faut d'abord imaginer.
author: From \"La courbe\"

text: Style, tone, taste — the dimensions most AI evaluations refuse to look at.
author: After Contralabs

text: Taste is a muscle, not a personality trait.
Texts aren't products. They are traces of process. What you ship is the process itself, instrumented for someone else to walk into.
author: After Crooked Timber

text: The companies optimizing for taste, not throughput, will look very different in five years.
author: Music Business Worldwide

text: The films aren’t the asset — the workflow is.
author: IndieWire · Jun 24, 2026

text: The limit does the editing, and the discipline is in what you refuse to add.
author: Pitchfork, on Sad13

text: The narrowing is the craft.
author: null

text: To draw you in dark resin on the back of my forehead, so I can find you each time I close my eyes.
author: From \"The art of the capture\"

text: Traditional is a verb, not a passport.
author: On Marisa Anderson, Bandcamp Daily

text: Vermeer a compris que l'œil ne lit pas une texture, il lit la lumière qui s'y accroche.
author: null

text: Waste is a design choice.
author: David Byrne, via Kottke

text: Writing isn't sentence production. It is the training of endurance through sustained attention.
author: Micah Nathan, via Robin Rendle

text: You are no longer the user. You are the principal.
author: Sidebar
